prompt: Implement a DealerOps Offline Mobility & Inventory Suite for independent auto dealerships that need on-lot operations, sales lead capture, and staff compliance in a fully offline iOS environment. The app supports iPhone and iPad with a responsive UIKit interface that adapts to portrait/landscape, Split View on iPad, Safe Area, Auto Layout, Dark Mode support, and Dynamic Type scaling. Users sign in with a local username and password only; after the first successful login on the device, FaceID/TouchID can be enabled via LocalAuthentication for quick re-entry and forced re-authentication after 5 minutes in the background. Roles include Administrator (full configuration and audit review), Sales Associate (quotes/appointments and lead notes), Inventory Clerk (counts and variance prep), and Compliance Reviewer (exceptions and appeals). A unified intake form captures quote requests, test-drive or vehicle-viewing appointments, and general merchant contact, storing customer name, phone (e.g., 415-555-0123), preferred contact window, vehicle interest, and consent notes; each lead follows a workflow of New → Follow-Up → Closed-Won → Invalid, with assignment, tagged notes, and reminders. SLA rules generate on-device alerts when a New lead is not updated within 2 business hours or a scheduled appointment is not confirmed within 30 minutes of its start time. For internal transportation/courtesy-ride needs, carpool matching lets staff create pooling orders and matches riders by 15-minute time windows, a configurable pickup radius defaulting to 2.0 miles, seat availability, and a detour threshold of 10% or 1.5 miles (whichever is smaller), using a route-overlap score to merge multi-passenger trips; location input is manual or from last-known device location with battery optimization by limiting updates and avoiding continuous tracking. Exception and appeal closed loop automatically flags missed check-ins, suspected buddy punching, and misidentification; users can file an appeal with notes and evidence attachments, routed to a Reviewer for approve/deny and written back to the original record. All data persists on-device using Core Data with well-defined entities such as User, Role, PermissionScope (by lot/site, date range, and function), Lead, Appointment, Reminder, PoolOrder, RouteSegment, InventoryItem, CountTask, CountBatch, Variance, AdjustmentOrder, ExceptionCase, Appeal, EvidenceFile, and AuditLog. Inventory counting supports task assignment, batch-based counts, and optional external scanner input as plain text identifiers; counts automatically compare expected vs. counted quantities and detect surplus, shortage, location mismatch, and custodian mismatch, requiring Admin approval for variances over ±3 units or ±2% (whichever is greater) before generating an adjustment order. Images/videos used as evidence or asset records are stored in the app sandbox with size limits (50 MB per video, 10 MB per image), basic format validation and SHA-256 fingerprinting, optional visible watermarking (“DealerOps • Confidential”), and hotlink protection by design since files never leave the device; lifecycle rules auto-archive closed leads after 180 days and purge rejected appeal media after 30 days unless pinned by an Admin. Security and privacy requirements include encrypting sensitive fields at rest using iOS Data Protection and per-record encryption keys stored in Keychain, masking phone numbers in list views (e.g., ***-***-0123), least-privilege role checks for every screen, and deletable yet traceable audit logs (tombstone entries retained for 1 year). The app must cold-start to an interactive home screen in under 1.5 seconds on iPhone 11-class hardware, handle memory warnings by releasing media caches, and use Background Tasks to run deferred matching/variance calculations and scheduled cleanup while respecting battery constraints and remaining fully functional with no network connectivity or external services. Minimum supported iOS version is 15.0.
